label: Ethene π bond (sp² C=C)
description: Each carbon is sp²; one unhybridised p orbital on each C overlaps sideways to form the π bond.
teacher_note: The σ framework lies in a plane; the π bond is perpendicular.

label: Acetylene sp (C≡C)
description: Each carbon in acetylene is sp hybridised; two π bonds are formed by two pairs of perpendicular p orbitals.

label: Formaldehyde carbonyl π bond
description: Both C and O in the C=O are sp² hybridised; the p orbitals overlap to form the π bond.
teacher_note: Oxygen is more electronegative so the π bond is polarised toward O.

label: Benzene aromatic π cloud
description: All six carbons are sp²; the six p orbitals combine to form the delocalised π cloud above and below the ring.
teacher_note: Hückel 4n+2 rule: benzene has 6 π electrons (n=1).

label: 1,3-Butadiene conjugated π system
description: Four adjacent sp² carbons; the four p orbitals form a conjugated π system spanning all four carbons.
teacher_note: Conjugation lowers energy and extends UV absorption wavelength.

label: Allyl cation (allylic stabilisation)
description: The empty p orbital on the allylic cation delocalises with the adjacent π bond, spreading the positive charge over three carbons.
teacher_note: Allylic stabilisation ~ 15 kcal/mol more stable than a simple 2° carbocation.

label: Pyridine aromatic π system
description: Pyridine is isoelectronic with benzene; nitrogen is sp² and contributes its p orbital to the 6-π-electron aromatic system.
teacher_note: The nitrogen lone pair is in an sp² orbital in the ring plane, NOT in the π system.

label: Furan aromatic π system
description: Furan has 6 π electrons: 4 from the two double bonds and 2 from the oxygen lone pair in the p orbital.
teacher_note: Oxygen lone pair IS part of the π system here (contrast with pyridine).

label: Allene orthogonal π systems
description: The central carbon of allene is sp hybridised; the two π bonds are perpendicular to each other.
teacher_note: Allene is chiral if the two ends bear different substituents.
